data.text: The direct deadcode run confirms the tree still has unrelated unreachable code, but I only care about the evidence package here. I’m narrowing to that package so I can tell whether these 8 symbols are still live, and if not, whether they need a production anchor or a deletion.

The reachability scan came back clean for `internal/evidence`, which means the package may already be wired in this worktree. I’m checking the git state now; if there are no source changes needed, I’ll still produce the required decisions log and use the no-change path only if the tracker expects an explicit rationale artifact.

`git status` is clean, so there’s no partially done work to rescue. I’m letting the full `go test ./...` finish first, then I’ll write the decisions log and decide whether this is a pure evidence commit or whether a source patch is actually needed.

`go test ./...` is not green on the current baseline: I’m seeing failures in `cli/cmd` that are unrelated to the evidence package. I’m waiting for the run to finish so I can tell whether this is a single flake or a broader blocker before deciding between a no-change rationale and a narrow fix.

I’ve got enough evidence to write the decision log now. Before I do, I’m confirming whether `AssembleRefOnly` actually exists in this tree, because the symbol list says it should and I don’t want to invent a decision for a function that’s already gone.

I’m writing the evidence artifacts now: a one-line-per-symbol decisions log, plus a `needs_investigation` rationale because the repo’s own `go test ./...` is failing outside this bead’s scope. No source edits yet.
I’ve recorded the artifacts and I’m doing one last sanity check on the diff/status so I can hand back an exact blocker instead of a vague partial.
